themes: Services at Higher Levels of Health System

Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.remarks: The school eye health program is implemented by the relevant Ministries of Education and Health with support from implementing partners. | No, there is no policy or regulation to limit vision screening by teachers in the school.

Operationalization Strategies.remarks: Training manuals are designed to help teachers understand and deliver integrated school health interventions. The manual provides guidelines for teachers to conduct vision screening using available resources. | Teachers and support staff have benefited from school eye health training on how to implement screening in schools.

Coordination and Integration.remarks: The school eye health program is implemented through coordination between Ministries of Education and Health with support from implementing partners. Training manuals provide an integrated approach to school health interventions.

Accessibility and Inclusivity.remarks: Children who are found to have more serious eye conditions are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. Teachers may also be screened for refractive errors and provided with glasses according to their needs.

Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.remarks: Children who are found to have more serious eye conditions are referred to an appropriate health facility for advanced treatment and care. Trained eye health professionals provide specialized services at hospitals.

Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.enabler_keywords: Ministry, Government, plan, policy + delivered, included, aligned, partnership, stakeholders
Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.enabler_remarks: Programme is delivered in partnership with relevant Ministries and is aligned with national sector plans and health policies. School health is included in the National Education Policy. Key stakeholders and coordination mechanisms are established between ministries.
Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.barrier_remarks: No IPEC Plan exists. Rehabilitation or Assistive Technology Policy/Strategy/Plan does not exist.
Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.strengths: School health policy guides the planning and implementation of school health programmes.

Operationalization Strategies.enabler_keywords: Sector, sectoral, plan + aligned, partnership, train
Operationalization Strategies.enabler_remarks: Programme is delivered in partnership with relevant Ministries and is aligned with national education and health sector plans.
Operationalization Strategies.strengths: The existence of a technical working group serves as a platform for coordination of school eye health programmes.

Coordination and Integration.enabler_remarks: Coordination mechanisms exist between ministries of health and education through technical working groups for integrated eye health services.
Coordination and Integration.barrier_remarks: No IPEC Plan exists. Rehabilitation or Assistive Technology Policy/Strategy/Plan does not exist.
Coordination and Integration.strengths: The existence of a technical working group serves as a platform for coordination of school eye health programmes. Alignment of programme approach with national sector plans facilitates acceptance and ownership.
Coordination and Integration.limitations: Absence of a systematic integrated approach to planning.

Funding.enabler_keywords: fund, budget, finance + allocated, working with
Funding.enabler_remarks: Budget allocated for school health/eye health by relevant ministries. Health financing mechanisms include national budget, bilateral/multilateral funding and NGO support.
Funding.barrier_keywords: inadequate, not included, limited + finance(ial), budget, investment
Funding.barrier_remarks: Inadequate state investment in school health services. Eyeglasses not included in health insurance or other state financing mechanisms. Limited financial resources and competing priorities.
Funding.limitations: Inadequate funding and budgeting for smooth implementation of school health programmes.

Accessibility and Inclusivity.enabler_keywords: included, provide + disability, special needs, special education, social policy
Accessibility and Inclusivity.enabler_remarks: School health policy is included in Disability policy and Special Needs/Special Education frameworks. Social Protection Policy includes provisions for school health.

Data Considerations.limitations: Lack of epidemiological information on eye health required to plan and evaluate strategies.

Procuring Eyeglasses.barrier_remarks: Eyeglasses not included in health insurance or other state financing mechanisms.

Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.enabler_keywords: (institutional body, departments), national + comprised, set up
Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.enabler_remarks: Data is shared through coordination meetings at national levels between departments of Education and Health.

Operationalization Strategies.enabler_keywords: working group + stakeholder + set up, comprised
Operationalization Strategies.enabler_remarks: A Technical Working Group is set up comprised of Education, Health workers and community representatives.
Operationalization Strategies.strengths: Institutionalization of monitoring and evaluation frameworks. Strengthened referral pathways to ensure continuum of care.
Operationalization Strategies.limitations: Absence of school eye health indicators in EMIS and HMIS to monitor progress. Regulation and supervision of school health programmes across stakeholders.

Coordination and Integration.enabler_remarks: Coordination meetings at national levels where data is shared between departments of Education and Health.
Coordination and Integration.strengths: Proper planning and coordination of activities.
Coordination and Integration.limitations: Weak coordination among stakeholders of school health within the intervention area.

Funding.barrier_remarks: No specific indicators on school health or eye health within the Education Management Information System. The EMIS system is underfunded.

Local Capacity and Service Delivery.enabler_keywords: primary, community + available, provide + intervention area
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.enabler_remarks: Primary eye care staff are available with necessary skills for basic screening and detecting eye health conditions. Primary eye care services are linked with school health services. Health screening activities are undertaken in the community.
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.barrier_remarks: Monitoring and supportive supervision takes place but needs to be strengthened.

Cost, Availability and Affordability.barrier_remarks: No financial cost information for eye care services offered.

Data Considerations.enabler_remarks: Data is shared through coordination meetings at national levels between departments.
Data Considerations.barrier_remarks: Monitoring and supportive supervision needs to be strengthened. No specific indicators linked to school eye health in management information systems.

Sociocultural Factors and Compliance.enabler_keywords: health education, reminders + parents + enable, sent
Sociocultural Factors and Compliance.enabler_remarks: Health education and promotion to teachers and parents enables understanding of eye health benefits. Reminders sent to parents on follow up appointments.

Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.enabler_remarks: Secondary and tertiary eye care facilities are available. Secondary eye care staff have necessary skills. Referral pathways exist from school screening to specialist care.
Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.barrier_remarks: No specific indicators linked to school eye health at district level reporting.

Procuring Eyeglasses.barrier_remarks: Availability of eyeglasses for children affected by supply chain issues.

Operationalization Strategies.enabler_keywords: workshop, seminars, train(ing) + there is, used + health worker, teacher
Operationalization Strategies.enabler_remarks: Training manuals are used to train both teachers and health workers. Opportunities for knowledge enhancement through workshops and seminars.
Operationalization Strategies.barrier_keywords: plan, monitor, training + human resource, health worker + limited, needed
Operationalization Strategies.barrier_remarks: Additional in-service training needed for specialist eye care roles. Essential health human resources are limited. Regular refresher trainings needed for teachers and school administrators.
Operationalization Strategies.limitations: Absence of epidemiological data to plan and evaluate strategies. Poor planning.

Local Capacity and Service Delivery.enabler_keywords: available, there is, implement + intervention area
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.enabler_remarks: Essential health human resources are available in the education sector to implement school health programs.
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.barrier_keywords: limited staff, needed, to conduct, supervision + intervention area
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.barrier_remarks: Essential health human resources are limited and aging. Additional training needed for specialist roles. Supportive supervision needs strengthening.
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.strengths: Capacity development of teachers and health workers based on gaps identified. Efficient and effective use of human resources.

Data Considerations.strengths: Accurate information on disease burden, quality and quantity of human resources provided to policymakers and health care authorities.

Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.enabler_remarks: Health regulatory agencies regulate and direct the import and procurement of eyeglasses.
Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.barrier_keywords: polic(ies) + do not cover | Ministry + not included
Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.barrier_remarks: Most policy mechanisms do not cover the cost of vision or refraction. Eyeglasses not included on Essential Supplies List.
Institutional Structure and Stakeholders.limitations: Inclusion of eyeglasses on Essential Supplies List could facilitate importation and affordability.

Local Capacity and Service Delivery.enabler_remarks: Anti-biotic eye drops/ointment are available at primary health care level. Standards exist to detect, diagnose and treat school age children at various levels.
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.barrier_remarks: People find it difficult to pay for glasses. Most people struggle financially.
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.limitations: Some facilities do not have the equipment required to detect and treat school age children.

Accessibility and Inclusivity.limitations: People from the lowest income groups cannot afford spectacles despite subsidization efforts.

Cost, Availability and Affordability.barrier_keywords: cost + do not cover, difficult to pay
Cost, Availability and Affordability.barrier_remarks: Most policy mechanisms do not cover the cost of vision or refraction or eyeglasses. People find it difficult to pay for glasses.
Cost, Availability and Affordability.strengths: Availability of ophthalmic equipment to detect and treat school age children. Constant supply of consumables.
Cost, Availability and Affordability.limitations: Inclusion of eyeglasses on Essential Supplies List could improve affordability. Lowest income groups cannot afford spectacles.

Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.enabler_keywords: consumables, equipment + available, there is + secondary level, tertiary level
Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.enabler_remarks: Anti-biotic eye drops/ointment and diagnostic equipment available at secondary and tertiary levels.

Procuring Eyeglasses.enabler_keywords: eyeglasses, spectacle frames, lenses, optical product + procure(ment), set up
Procuring Eyeglasses.enabler_remarks: Health regulatory agencies regulate procurement. Optical products set up in health facilities by partners.
Procuring Eyeglasses.barrier_remarks: Eyeglasses not included on Essential Supplies List.
Procuring Eyeglasses.strengths: Availability of spectacles at public health facilities with a wide range of frames could address compliance and cost challenges.
Procuring Eyeglasses.limitations: Inclusion of eyeglasses on Essential Supplies List could improve affordability. Lowest income groups cannot afford spectacles.

Funding.strengths: Adequate funding for school eye health programmes.

Local Capacity and Service Delivery.keywords: lack of, limited staff + No + intervention area
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.remarks: No eyeglasses suitable for children available within the intervention area due to supply chain issues.
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.strengths: Task reallocation (training of teachers to screen and refer).
Local Capacity and Service Delivery.limitations: Shortage of skilled eye health workers.

Cost, Availability and Affordability.limitations: Availability and affordability of eye health services. Supply chain challenges that affect cost, range (frames) and availability of spectacles.

Sociocultural Factors and Compliance.remarks: Barriers to wearing eyeglasses include low literacy levels of parents, fears about wearing eyeglasses, misinformation and misconceptions, cultural beliefs and practices. There is a need for more community awareness about school eye health programs.
Sociocultural Factors and Compliance.limitations: Lack of information and awareness of the benefits of eye health. Cultural perceptions.

Services at Higher Levels of Health Syst.remarks: Services provided to children with vision impairment issues. Lack of eye care professionals to receive and attend to referrals due to limited staff.

Procuring Eyeglasses.keywords:
Eyeglasses + not produce(d)
No + eyeglasses + available
Procuring Eyeglasses.remarks: No eyeglasses suitable for children available within the intervention area due to supply chain issues. Glasses are imported and not produced in country.
Procuring Eyeglasses.limitations: Supply chain challenges that affect cost, range (frames) and availability of spectacles.
